---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---



```{r}
# ============================================================
# Eye Tracking — Multi-Poster Multi-Participant Plot Generator
# ============================================================
# Folder structure output:
#   output/
#     poster_ArminHofman/
#       p1/
#         heatmap_plot.png
#         gaze_path_plot.png
#         scanpath_plot.png
#         gaze_animation.gif
#       p2/ ...
#     poster_BarryCeck/ ...

library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(jpeg)
library(png)
library(grid)
library(gridExtra)
library(MASS)       # for kde2d (heatmap)
library(gganimate)  # for gif
library(gifski)     # gif renderer

# ============================================================
# CONFIGURATION — adjust these paths if needed
# ============================================================
ROOT         <- "."                   # repo root; run from Eye-Tracker/
SIGNALS_DIR  <- file.path(ROOT, "Renamed Signals")
POSTERS_DIR  <- file.path(ROOT, "posters")
OUTPUT_DIR   <- file.path(ROOT, "output")

# Column name mapping — adjust to match your actual CSV columns
COL_X        <- "ET_GazeLeftx"        # raw gaze X
COL_Y        <- "ET_GazeLefty"        # raw gaze Y
COL_TIME     <- "Timestamp"           # time in ms
COL_FIX_X   <- "ET_FixationPointX"   # fixation X (for scanpath)
COL_FIX_Y   <- "ET_FixationPointY"   # fixation Y
COL_FIX_DUR <- "ET_FixationDuration" # fixation duration ms
COL_FIX_IDX <- "ET_FixationIndex"    # fixation order index
COL_SLIDE   <- "SlideEvent"          # slide/stimulus name column (in SlideEvents CSV)

# ============================================================
# HELPERS
# ============================================================

#' Load the background poster image as a raster grob
load_poster <- function(poster_path) {
  ext <- tolower(tools::file_ext(poster_path))
  img <- if (ext %in% c("jpg", "jpeg")) readJPEG(poster_path) else readPNG(poster_path)
  rasterGrob(img, width = unit(1, "npc"), height = unit(1, "npc"), interpolate = TRUE)
}

#' Load and filter gaze data for one participant + one poster
#' Returns a data frame with gaze/fixation columns
load_participant_data <- function(participant_dir, poster_name) {
  # Read the main ET_RExtAPI file
  et_file <- list.files(participant_dir, pattern = "ET_RExtAPI.*\\.csv$",
                        full.names = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)
  slide_file <- list.files(participant_dir, pattern = "Native_SlideEvents.*\\.csv$",
                           full.names = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)

  if (length(et_file) == 0) {
    warning("No ET_RExtAPI CSV found in: ", participant_dir)
    return(NULL)
  }

  df <- read_csv(et_file[1], show_col_types = FALSE)

  # If a SlideEvents file exists, use it to get timestamp range for this poster
  if (length(slide_file) > 0) {
    slides <- read_csv(slide_file[1], show_col_types = FALSE)

    # Try to find the row matching this poster (flexible name match)
    poster_rows <- slides %>%
      filter(str_detect(tolower(.data[[names(slides)[1]]]),
                        tolower(str_replace_all(poster_name, "[^a-zA-Z0-9]", ""))))

    if (nrow(poster_rows) >= 1) {
      # Assumes slides have start/end time columns — adapt column names as needed
      time_cols <- names(slides)
      # Common iMotions column names for slide timing:
      start_col <- intersect(c("SlideStartTime", "EventTime", "Timestamp"), time_cols)[1]
      end_col   <- intersect(c("SlideEndTime", "EventEndTime"), time_cols)[1]

      if (!is.na(start_col) && !is.na(end_col)) {
        t_start <- poster_rows[[start_col]][1]
        t_end   <- poster_rows[[end_col]][1]
        df <- df %>% filter(.data[[COL_TIME]] >= t_start & .data[[COL_TIME]] <= t_end)
      }
    }
  }

  # Drop rows with missing gaze
  df <- df %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[COL_X]]), !is.na(.data[[COL_Y]]),
           .data[[COL_X]] > 0, .data[[COL_Y]] > 0)

  df
}

# ============================================================
# PLOT 1 — Raw Gaze Path (colour = time progression)
# ============================================================
plot_gaze_path <- function(df, bg_grob, img_w, img_h, out_path) {
  # Normalise coordinates to [0,1] if they are in pixels
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(
      x_norm = .data[[COL_X]] / img_w,
      y_norm = 1 - (.data[[COL_Y]] / img_h),  # flip Y (screen vs plot coords)
      t_norm = (.data[[COL_TIME]] - min(.data[[COL_TIME]])) /
               (max(.data[[COL_TIME]]) - min(.data[[COL_TIME]]))
    )

  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = x_norm, y = y_norm, colour = .data[[COL_TIME]])) +
    annotation_custom(bg_grob, xmin = 0, xmax = 1, ymin = 0, ymax = 1) +
    geom_path(linewidth = 0.6, alpha = 0.85) +
    geom_point(size = 0.8, alpha = 0.5) +
    scale_colour_viridis_c(option = "plasma", name = "Time (ms)",
                           labels = scales::comma) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), expand = c(0, 0)) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), expand = c(0, 0)) +
    coord_fixed(ratio = img_h / img_w) +
    labs(title = "Eye Tracking \u2014 Raw Gaze Path",
         subtitle = "Colour = time progression (early \u2192 late)") +
    theme_void() +
    theme(
      plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA),
      plot.title       = element_text(colour = "white", hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle    = element_text(colour = "grey70", hjust = 0.5, size = 9),
      legend.position  = "bottom",
      legend.title     = element_text(colour = "white", size = 9),
      legend.text      = element_text(colour = "white", size = 8),
      legend.key.width = unit(2, "cm")
    )

  ggsave(out_path, p, width = 13, height = 9, dpi = 150, bg = "black")
  message("  Saved: ", out_path)
}

# ============================================================
# PLOT 2 — Gaze Density Heatmap
# ============================================================
plot_heatmap <- function(df, bg_grob, img_w, img_h, out_path) {
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(
      x_norm = .data[[COL_X]] / img_w,
      y_norm = 1 - (.data[[COL_Y]] / img_h)
    ) %>%
    filter(x_norm >= 0, x_norm <= 1, y_norm >= 0, y_norm <= 1)

  # KDE
  kde <- kde2d(df$x_norm, df$y_norm, n = 200,
               lims = c(0, 1, 0, 1))
  kde_df <- expand.grid(x = kde$x, y = kde$y) %>%
    mutate(density = as.vector(kde$z))

  p <- ggplot() +
    annotation_custom(bg_grob, xmin = 0, xmax = 1, ymin = 0, ymax = 1) +
    geom_raster(data = kde_df, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = density), alpha = 0.65) +
    scale_fill_gradientn(
      colours = c("transparent", "#0000FF44", "#00FFFF88",
                  "#00FF00CC", "#FFFF00EE", "#FF0000FF"),
      name = "Gaze Density"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), expand = c(0, 0)) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), expand = c(0, 0)) +
    coord_fixed(ratio = img_h / img_w) +
    labs(title = "Eye Tracking \u2014 Gaze Density Heatmap",
         subtitle = "Warmer colours = more time spent looking") +
    theme_void() +
    theme(
      plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA),
      plot.title       = element_text(colour = "white", hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle    = element_text(colour = "grey70", hjust = 0.5, size = 9),
      legend.position  = "bottom",
      legend.title     = element_text(colour = "white", size = 9),
      legend.text      = element_text(colour = "white", size = 8),
      legend.key.width = unit(2, "cm")
    )

  ggsave(out_path, p, width = 13, height = 9, dpi = 150, bg = "black")
  message("  Saved: ", out_path)
}

# ============================================================
# PLOT 3 — Scanpath (fixations + saccades)
# ============================================================
plot_scanpath <- function(df, bg_grob, img_w, img_h, out_path) {
  # Check fixation columns exist
  needed <- c(COL_FIX_X, COL_FIX_Y, COL_FIX_DUR, COL_FIX_IDX)
  missing <- needed[!needed %in% names(df)]
  if (length(missing) > 0) {
    warning("Scanpath: missing columns: ", paste(missing, collapse = ", "))
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  fix_df <- df %>%
    select(fix_x   = all_of(COL_FIX_X),
           fix_y   = all_of(COL_FIX_Y),
           dur     = all_of(COL_FIX_DUR),
           fix_idx = all_of(COL_FIX_IDX)) %>%
    filter(!is.na(fix_x), !is.na(fix_y), !is.na(fix_idx)) %>%
    distinct(fix_idx, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
    arrange(fix_idx) %>%
    mutate(
      x_norm = fix_x / img_w,
      y_norm = 1 - (fix_y / img_h)
    ) %>%
    filter(x_norm >= 0, x_norm <= 1, y_norm >= 0, y_norm <= 1)

  p <- ggplot(fix_df, aes(x = x_norm, y = y_norm)) +
    annotation_custom(bg_grob, xmin = 0, xmax = 1, ymin = 0, ymax = 1) +
    # saccade lines
    geom_path(colour = "white", linewidth = 0.4, alpha = 0.7) +
    # fixation circles sized by duration
    geom_point(aes(size = dur), colour = "#56B4E9", alpha = 0.75, shape = 16) +
    # fixation order labels
    geom_text(aes(label = fix_idx), colour = "white", size = 2.2, fontface = "bold") +
    scale_size_continuous(name = "Fixation Duration (ms)",
                          range = c(4, 14),
                          breaks = c(100, 150, 200, 250)) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), expand = c(0, 0)) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), expand = c(0, 0)) +
    coord_fixed(ratio = img_h / img_w) +
    labs(title = "Eye Tracking \u2014 Scanpath",
         subtitle = "Circles = fixation duration | Lines = saccades | Numbers = fixation order") +
    theme_void() +
    theme(
      plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA),
      plot.title       = element_text(colour = "white", hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle    = element_text(colour = "grey70", hjust = 0.5, size = 9),
      legend.position  = "bottom",
      legend.title     = element_text(colour = "white", size = 9),
      legend.text      = element_text(colour = "white", size = 8)
    )

  ggsave(out_path, p, width = 13, height = 9, dpi = 150, bg = "black")
  message("  Saved: ", out_path)
}

# ============================================================
# PLOT 4 — Gaze Animation (GIF)
# ============================================================
plot_animation <- function(df, bg_grob, img_w, img_h, out_path) {
  # Bin time into frames to keep gif manageable
  n_frames <- 80

  df_anim <- df %>%
    mutate(
      x_norm  = .data[[COL_X]] / img_w,
      y_norm  = 1 - (.data[[COL_Y]] / img_h),
      t_rank  = ntile(.data[[COL_TIME]], n_frames)
    ) %>%
    filter(x_norm >= 0, x_norm <= 1, y_norm >= 0, y_norm <= 1)

  # Cumulative path up to each frame
  anim_frames <- map_dfr(seq_len(n_frames), function(f) {
    df_anim %>% filter(t_rank <= f) %>% mutate(frame = f)
  })

  current_dot <- df_anim %>% rename(frame = t_rank)

  p <- ggplot() +
    annotation_custom(bg_grob, xmin = 0, xmax = 1, ymin = 0, ymax = 1) +
    geom_path(data = anim_frames,
              aes(x = x_norm, y = y_norm, group = 1, colour = frame),
              linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.8) +
    geom_point(data = current_dot,
               aes(x = x_norm, y = y_norm),
               colour = "white", size = 3, alpha = 0.9) +
    scale_colour_viridis_c(option = "plasma", guide = "none") +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), expand = c(0, 0)) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), expand = c(0, 0)) +
    coord_fixed(ratio = img_h / img_w) +
    labs(title = "Eye Tracking \u2014 Gaze Animation") +
    theme_void() +
    theme(
      plot.background = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA),
      plot.title      = element_text(colour = "white", hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold")
    ) +
    transition_time(frame) +
    ease_aes("linear")

  anim <- animate(p,
                  nframes    = n_frames,
                  fps        = 12,
                  width      = 1300,
                  height     = 900,
                  renderer   = gifski_renderer(),
                  bg         = "black")

  anim_save(out_path, anim)
  message("  Saved: ", out_path)
}

# ============================================================
# MAIN LOOP
# ============================================================
run_all <- function() {
  # Discover posters
  poster_files <- list.files(POSTERS_DIR, pattern = "\\.(jpg|jpeg|png|JPG|JPEG|PNG)$",
                             full.names = TRUE)
  poster_names <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(poster_files))

  # Discover participants (folders named p1, p2, ... inside Renamed Signals)
  participant_dirs <- list.dirs(SIGNALS_DIR, recursive = FALSE)
  participant_ids  <- basename(participant_dirs)

  message("Found ", length(poster_files), " posters and ",
          length(participant_dirs), " participants.\n")

  for (pi in seq_along(poster_files)) {
    poster_path <- poster_files[pi]
    poster_name <- poster_names[pi]

    message("=== Poster: ", poster_name, " ===")

    # Load poster image and get dimensions
    ext <- tolower(tools::file_ext(poster_path))
    img <- if (ext %in% c("jpg", "jpeg")) readJPEG(poster_path) else readPNG(poster_path)
    img_h <- dim(img)[1]
    img_w <- dim(img)[2]
    bg_grob <- rasterGrob(img, width = unit(1, "npc"), height = unit(1, "npc"),
                          interpolate = TRUE)

    for (di in seq_along(participant_dirs)) {
      part_dir <- participant_dirs[di]
      part_id  <- participant_ids[di]

      message("  Participant: ", part_id)

      # Output folder
      out_folder <- file.path(OUTPUT_DIR,
                              paste0("poster_", str_replace_all(poster_name, " ", "")),
                              part_id)
      dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

      # Load data
      df <- tryCatch(
        load_participant_data(part_dir, poster_name),
        error = function(e) { warning(e$message); NULL }
      )

      if (is.null(df) || nrow(df) < 10) {
        message("    Skipping — insufficient data")
        next
      }

      # Generate plots
      tryCatch(plot_gaze_path(df, bg_grob, img_w, img_h,
                              file.path(out_folder, "gaze_path_plot.png")),
               error = function(e) warning("gaze_path: ", e$message))

      tryCatch(plot_heatmap(df, bg_grob, img_w, img_h,
                            file.path(out_folder, "heatmap_plot.png")),
               error = function(e) warning("heatmap: ", e$message))

      tryCatch(plot_scanpath(df, bg_grob, img_w, img_h,
                             file.path(out_folder, "scanpath_plot.png")),
               error = function(e) warning("scanpath: ", e$message))

      tryCatch(plot_animation(df, bg_grob, img_w, img_h,
                              file.path(out_folder, "gaze_animation.gif")),
               error = function(e) warning("animation: ", e$message))
    }
  }

  message("\nDone! All plots saved to: ", OUTPUT_DIR)
}

# Run it
run_all()
```
